predicate_mappings.assigned_source_branch: the backport request {} is assigned to the source branch {}
predicate_mappings.has_assigned_source_branch: the backport request {} has an assigned source branch
predicate_mappings.approval_obtained: approval has been obtained for the backport request {}
predicate_mappings.validation_succeeded: validation succeeded for the backport request {}
predicate_mappings.associated_patch_commit: the backport request {} is associated with the patch commit {}
predicate_mappings.associated_component: the backport request {} is associated with the repository component {}

predicate_mappings.clearance_recorded: the clearance for the backport request {} is recorded

predicate_mappings.bound_release_window: the backport request {} is bound to the release window {}

preconditions: I can only open a backport request A if it is not the case that the backport request A is open and it is not the case that the request A is completed
effects: Once I open a backport request A, it is the case that the backport request A is open

propose_source_branch.description: propose a source branch candidate B for a backport request A
preconditions: I can only propose a source branch candidate B for a backport request A if it is the case that the backport request A is open and the source branch B is available and the request candidate B is compatible with the backport request A
I can only propose a source branch candidate B for a backport request A if it is not the case that the backport request A has an assigned source branch
effects: Once I propose a source branch candidate B for a backport request A, it is the case that the backport request A is assigned to the source branch B and the backport request A has an assigned source branch
Once I propose a source branch candidate B for a backport request A, it is not the case anymore that the source branch B is available

revoke_source_branch_assignment.description: revoke assignment of a source branch candidate B from a backport request A
preconditions: I can only revoke assignment of a source branch candidate B from a backport request A if it is the case that the backport request A is assigned to the source branch B

I can only revoke assignment of a source branch candidate B from a backport request A if it is not the case that the evaluation of the backport request A is completed and it is not the case that the clearance for the backport request A is recorded
effects: Once I revoke assignment of a source branch candidate B from a backport request A, it is the case that the source branch B is available

Once I revoke assignment of a source branch candidate B from a backport request A, it is not the case anymore that the backport request A is assigned to the source branch B and it is not the case anymore that the backport request A has an assigned source branch and it is not the case anymore that approval has been obtained for the backport request A and it is not the case anymore that validation succeeded for the backport request A and it is not the case anymore that A is pending additional checks and it is not the case anymore that A is ready for completion and it is not the case anymore that evaluation resources are allocated for A

bind_release_window.description: bind a backport request A to a release window B
preconditions: I can only bind a backport request A to a release window B if it is the case that the backport request A is open and the release window B is available
effects: Once I bind a backport request A to a release window B, it is the case that the backport request A is bound to the release window B
Once I bind a backport request A to a release window B, it is not the case anymore that the release window B is available

unbind_release_window.description: unbind a backport request A from a release window B
preconditions: I can only unbind a backport request A from a release window B if it is the case that the backport request A is bound to the release window B
effects: Once I unbind a backport request A from a release window B, it is the case that the release window B is available
Once I unbind a backport request A from a release window B, it is not the case anymore that the backport request A is bound to the release window B

confirm_release_window_approval.description: confirm approval for a backport request A in a release window B
preconditions: I can only confirm approval for a backport request A in a release window B if it is the case that the backport request A is open and the backport request A has an assigned source branch and the backport request A is bound to the release window B
I can only confirm approval for a backport request A in a release window B if it is not the case that approval has been obtained for the backport request A
effects: Once I confirm approval for a backport request A in a release window B, it is the case that approval has been obtained for the backport request A

record_oncall_approval.description: record approval for a backport request A by an oncall approver B
preconditions: I can only record approval for a backport request A by an oncall approver B if it is the case that the backport request A is open and the backport request A has an assigned source branch and the on call approver B is available
I can only record approval for a backport request A by an oncall approver B if it is not the case that approval has been obtained for the backport request A
effects: Once I record approval for a backport request A by an oncall approver B, it is the case that approval has been obtained for the backport request A and A is pending additional checks
Once I record approval for a backport request A by an oncall approver B, it is not the case anymore that the on call approver B is available

record_ci_validation.description: record CI validation for a backport request A during a release window B using a ci job C
preconditions: I can only record CI validation for a backport request A during a release window B using a ci job C if it is the case that approval has been obtained for the backport request A and the backport request A has an assigned source branch and the backport request A is bound to the release window B and the ci job C is available
I can only record CI validation for a backport request A during a release window B using a ci job C if it is not the case that validation succeeded for the backport request A
effects: Once I record CI validation for a backport request A during a release window B using a ci job C, it is the case that validation succeeded for the backport request A
Once I record CI validation for a backport request A during a release window B using a ci job C, it is not the case anymore that A is pending additional checks

record_reviewer_validation.description: record reviewer validation for a backport request A by a reviewer B
preconditions: I can only record reviewer validation for a backport request A by a reviewer B if it is the case that the backport request A has an assigned source branch and B is attached to A
I can only record reviewer validation for a backport request A by a reviewer B if it is not the case that validation succeeded for the backport request A
effects: Once I record reviewer validation for a backport request A by a reviewer B, it is the case that validation succeeded for the backport request A
Once I record reviewer validation for a backport request A by a reviewer B, it is not the case anymore that A is pending additional checks

associate_patch_commit.description: associate a patch commit B with a backport request A
preconditions: I can only associate a patch commit B with a backport request A if it is the case that the backport request A is open and the patch commit B is available and the patch commit B is a candidate for the backport request A
effects: Once I associate a patch commit B with a backport request A, it is the case that the backport request A is associated with the patch commit B
Once I associate a patch commit B with a backport request A, it is not the case anymore that the patch commit B is available

dissociate_patch_commit.description: dissociate a patch commit B from a backport request A
preconditions: I can only dissociate a patch commit B from a backport request A if it is the case that the backport request A is associated with the patch commit B
effects: Once I dissociate a patch commit B from a backport request A, it is the case that the patch commit B is available
Once I dissociate a patch commit B from a backport request A, it is not the case anymore that the backport request A is associated with the patch commit B

associate_component.description: associate a repository component B with a backport request A
preconditions: I can only associate a repository component B with a backport request A if it is the case that the backport request A is open and the component B is available and the repository component B is a candidate for the backport request A
effects: Once I associate a repository component B with a backport request A, it is the case that the backport request A is associated with the repository component B
Once I associate a repository component B with a backport request A, it is not the case anymore that the component B is available

dissociate_component.description: dissociate a repository component B from a backport request A
preconditions: I can only dissociate a repository component B from a backport request A if it is the case that the backport request A is associated with the repository component B
effects: Once I dissociate a repository component B from a backport request A, it is the case that the component B is available
Once I dissociate a repository component B from a backport request A, it is not the case anymore that the backport request A is associated with the repository component B

associate_backport_candidate.description: associate a backport candidate commit B with a backport request A
preconditions: I can only associate a backport candidate commit B with a backport request A if it is the case that the backport request A is open and the backport candidate commit B is available and the backport candidate commit B is requested for backport in A
effects: Once I associate a backport candidate commit B with a backport request A, it is the case that the backport request A is associated with the backport candidate commit B
Once I associate a backport candidate commit B with a backport request A, it is not the case anymore that the backport candidate commit B is available

dissociate_backport_candidate.description: dissociate a backport candidate commit B from a backport request A
preconditions: I can only dissociate a backport candidate commit B from a backport request A if it is the case that the backport request A is associated with the backport candidate commit B
effects: Once I dissociate a backport candidate commit B from a backport request A, it is the case that the backport candidate commit B is available
Once I dissociate a backport candidate commit B from a backport request A, it is not the case anymore that the backport request A is associated with the backport candidate commit B

assign_risk_tag.description: assign a risk tag B to a backport request A
preconditions: I can only assign a risk tag B to a backport request A if it is the case that the backport request A is open and the risk tag B is available and the backport request A has the risk tag B
effects: Once I assign a risk tag B to a backport request A, it is the case that the backport request A is assigned the risk tag B
Once I assign a risk tag B to a backport request A, it is not the case anymore that the risk tag B is available

unassign_risk_tag.description: unassign a risk tag B from a backport request A
preconditions: I can only unassign a risk tag B from a backport request A if it is the case that the backport request A is assigned the risk tag B
effects: Once I unassign a risk tag B from a backport request A, it is the case that the risk tag B is available
Once I unassign a risk tag B from a backport request A, it is not the case anymore that the backport request A is assigned the risk tag B

execute_release_evaluation.description: execute release evaluation for a backport request A with a target release B, a patch commit C, and a repository component D
preconditions: I can only execute release evaluation for a backport request A with a target release B, a patch commit C, and a repository component D if it is the case that the backport request A has an assigned source branch and the backport request A is associated with the patch commit C and the backport request A is associated with the repository component D and the target release B is available and the request target B is compatible with the backport request A

I can only execute release evaluation for a backport request A with a target release B, a patch commit C, and a repository component D if it is not the case that the evaluation of the backport request A is completed
effects: Once I execute release evaluation for a backport request A with a target release B, a patch commit C, and a repository component D, it is the case that the backport request A has the selected target release B and the evaluation of the backport request A is completed
Once I execute release evaluation for a backport request A with a target release B, a patch commit C, and a repository component D, it is not the case anymore that the target release B is available

execute_environment_evaluation.description: execute environment evaluation for a backport request A with a target release B, a backport candidate commit C, and a test environment D
preconditions: I can only execute environment evaluation for a backport request A with a target release B, a backport candidate commit C, and a test environment D if it is the case that the backport request A has an assigned source branch and the backport request A is associated with the backport candidate commit C and the test environment D is available and the target release B is available and the request target B is compatible with the backport request A and the backport request A is testable in the environment D

I can only execute environment evaluation for a backport request A with a target release B, a backport candidate commit C, and a test environment D if it is not the case that the evaluation of the backport request A is completed
effects: Once I execute environment evaluation for a backport request A with a target release B, a backport candidate commit C, and a test environment D, it is the case that the backport request A has the selected target release B and the evaluation of the backport request A is completed and evaluation resources are allocated for A and A is pending additional checks

Once I execute environment evaluation for a backport request A with a target release B, a backport candidate commit C, and a test environment D, it is not the case anymore that the target release B is available and it is not the case anymore that the test environment D is available

acknowledge_evaluation_outcome.description: acknowledge evaluation outcome for a backport request A with a patch commit B in a repository component C
preconditions: I can only acknowledge evaluation outcome for a backport request A with a patch commit B in a repository component C if it is the case that the evaluation of the backport request A is completed and evaluation resources are allocated for A and the backport request A is associated with the patch commit B and the backport request A is associated with the repository component C
effects: Once I acknowledge evaluation outcome for a backport request A with a patch commit B in a repository component C, it is not the case anymore that evaluation resources are allocated for A and it is not the case anymore that A is pending additional checks

record_primary_approval.description: record primary approval for a backport request A with a patch commit B in a repository component C by a primary approver group D
preconditions: I can only record primary approval for a backport request A with a patch commit B in a repository component C by a primary approver group D if it is the case that validation succeeded for the backport request A and the evaluation of the backport request A is completed and the backport request A is associated with the patch commit B and the backport request A is associated with the repository component C and the backport request A requires the approver group D

I can only record primary approval for a backport request A with a patch commit B in a repository component C by a primary approver group D if it is not the case that A is pending additional checks and it is not the case that the clearance for the backport request A is recorded
effects: Once I record primary approval for a backport request A with a patch commit B in a repository component C by a primary approver group D, it is the case that the clearance for the backport request A is recorded

record_secondary_approval_and_escalation.description: record secondary approval and escalation for a backport request A with a backport candidate commit B tagged with a risk tag C by a secondary approver group D
preconditions: I can only record secondary approval and escalation for a backport request A with a backport candidate commit B tagged with a risk tag C by a secondary approver group D if it is the case that validation succeeded for the backport request A and the evaluation of the backport request A is completed and the backport request A is associated with the backport candidate commit B and the backport request A is assigned the risk tag C and the backport request A requires the approver group D

I can only record secondary approval and escalation for a backport request A with a backport candidate commit B tagged with a risk tag C by a secondary approver group D if it is not the case that the clearance for the backport request A is recorded
effects: Once I record secondary approval and escalation for a backport request A with a backport candidate commit B tagged with a risk tag C by a secondary approver group D, it is the case that the clearance for the backport request A is recorded and A is pending additional checks

finalize_approval_to_ready.description: finalize approval to ready for a backport request A with a patch commit B in a repository component C
preconditions: I can only finalize approval to ready for a backport request A with a patch commit B in a repository component C if it is the case that the clearance for the backport request A is recorded and A is pending additional checks and the backport request A is associated with the patch commit B and the backport request A is associated with the repository component C
effects: Once I finalize approval to ready for a backport request A with a patch commit B in a repository component C, it is the case that A is ready for completion

Once I finalize approval to ready for a backport request A with a patch commit B in a repository component C, it is not the case anymore that A is pending additional checks and it is not the case anymore that validation succeeded for the backport request A and it is not the case anymore that evaluation resources are allocated for A

revalidate_with_ci.description: revalidate a backport request A with a ci job C during a release window B
preconditions: I can only revalidate a backport request A with a ci job C during a release window B if it is the case that A is ready for completion and the clearance for the backport request A is recorded and the backport request A has an assigned source branch and the backport request A is bound to the release window B and the ci job C is available
I can only revalidate a backport request A with a ci job C during a release window B if it is not the case that validation succeeded for the backport request A
effects: Once I revalidate a backport request A with a ci job C during a release window B, it is the case that validation succeeded for the backport request A

authorize_review_for_release_window.description: authorize review for a backport request A in a release window B
preconditions: I can only authorize review for a backport request A in a release window B if it is the case that the clearance for the backport request A is recorded and validation succeeded for the backport request A and the backport request A is bound to the release window B

I can only authorize review for a backport request A in a release window B if it is not the case that A is pending additional checks and it is not the case that the review for the backport request A is authorized
effects: Once I authorize review for a backport request A in a release window B, it is the case that the review for the backport request A is authorized

authorize_review_by_oncall.description: authorize review for a backport request A by an oncall approver B
preconditions: I can only authorize review for a backport request A by an oncall approver B if it is the case that the clearance for the backport request A is recorded and validation succeeded for the backport request A and the on call approver B is available

I can only authorize review for a backport request A by an oncall approver B if it is not the case that A is pending additional checks and it is not the case that the review for the backport request A is authorized
effects: Once I authorize review for a backport request A by an oncall approver B, it is the case that the review for the backport request A is authorized
Once I authorize review for a backport request A by an oncall approver B, it is not the case anymore that the on call approver B is available

bind_reviewer_to_request.description: bind a reviewer B to a backport request A
preconditions: I can only bind a reviewer B to a backport request A if it is the case that the review for the backport request A is authorized and the reviewer B is available and the default reviewer for A is B
effects: Once I bind a reviewer B to a backport request A, it is the case that the backport request A is assigned to the reviewer B
Once I bind a reviewer B to a backport request A, it is not the case anymore that the reviewer B is available

assign_reviewer_for_variant.description: assign a reviewer C to a standard case variant A for an urgent case variant B
preconditions: I can only assign a reviewer C to a standard case variant A for an urgent case variant B if it is the case that the backport request A is open and the backport request A is a standard case and A has a case variant reviewer mapping with C and the backport request B is assigned to the reviewer C
I can only assign a reviewer C to a standard case variant A for an urgent case variant B if it is not the case that C is attached to A
effects: Once I assign a reviewer C to a standard case variant A for an urgent case variant B, it is the case that C is attached to A

record_validation_milestone.description: record validation milestone for a backport request A in a release window B using a ci job C
preconditions: I can only record validation milestone for a backport request A in a release window B using a ci job C if it is the case that the backport request A is open and the backport request A is a standard case and validation succeeded for the backport request A and the review for the backport request A is authorized and the backport request A is bound to the release window B and the ci job C is available
I can only record validation milestone for a backport request A in a release window B using a ci job C if it is not the case that the final validation is recorded for A
effects: Once I record validation milestone for a backport request A in a release window B using a ci job C, it is the case that the final validation is recorded for A

complete_request_urgent_flow.description: complete urgent flow for a backport request A
preconditions: I can only complete urgent flow for a backport request A if it is the case that the backport request A is declared urgent and the backport request A is open and the backport request A has an assigned source branch and the evaluation of the backport request A is completed and the clearance for the backport request A is recorded and the review for the backport request A is authorized and validation succeeded for the backport request A
I can only complete urgent flow for a backport request A if it is not the case that the request A is completed
effects: Once I complete urgent flow for a backport request A, it is the case that the request A is completed

complete_request_with_reviewer_approval.description: complete request for a backport request A with approval from a reviewer B
preconditions: I can only complete request for a backport request A with approval from a reviewer B if it is the case that the backport request A is a standard case and the backport request A is open and the backport request A has an assigned source branch and the evaluation of the backport request A is completed and the clearance for the backport request A is recorded and the review for the backport request A is authorized and validation succeeded for the backport request A and B is attached to A
I can only complete request for a backport request A with approval from a reviewer B if it is not the case that the request A is completed
effects: Once I complete request for a backport request A with approval from a reviewer B, it is the case that the request A is completed

complete_request_with_final_validation.description: complete request for a backport request A with final validation
preconditions: I can only complete request for a backport request A with final validation if it is the case that the backport request A is a standard case and the backport request A is open and the backport request A has an assigned source branch and the evaluation of the backport request A is completed and the clearance for the backport request A is recorded and the review for the backport request A is authorized and validation succeeded for the backport request A and the final validation is recorded for A
I can only complete request for a backport request A with final validation if it is not the case that the request A is completed
effects: Once I complete request for a backport request A with final validation, it is the case that the request A is completed

type_hierarchy: Everything that is a generic object is also a object

Everything that is a backport request or a source branch candidate or a target release or a repository component or a patch commit or a backport candidate commit or a release window or a oncall approver or a reviewer or a ci job or a test environment or a risk tag or a approver group is also a generic object
Everything that is a primary approver group or a secondary approver group is also a approver group
Everything that is a urgent case variant or a standard case variant is also a backport request
